summary: Add authenticated desktop workspace sync backed by CockroachDB and replace placeholder workspaces with user-created workspace records plus terminal board layout metadata.

review_items.summary: Adds user-owned workspaces with first-run terminal count and 4 by 2 terminal layout metadata.
Creates the workspaces table with user ownership, uniqueness, and terminal count constraints.
Adds /api/desktop/workspaces for authenticated desktop session GET, POST, and PATCH workspace sync.
The desktop app fetches workspaces from the protected API and prompts for the first workspace when none exist.
The desktop dashboard replaces the text-based Forge Console with terminal-like panes opened from top hotkeys, automatically reflowed on the 8-slot board, and saved after the layout settles.
The desktop workspace terminal_layout contract accepts any non-overlapping pane rectangle that fits inside the 4 by 2 board and may persist a bounded model id for provider terminal restart.

security_data_rules: Workspace API requests from rust-diffforge must include a valid desktop session bearer token.
The API must scope all workspace reads and writes to the authenticated desktop session user_id.
Workspace names must be bounded, trimmed, and non-empty before insert.
terminal_count must be constrained to 1 through 8 before insert.
terminal_layout must be constrained to non-overlapping pane metadata on a 4-column by 2-row board.
terminal_layout pane rectangles may use any width from 1 through 4 and any height from 1 through 2 as long as every pane remains inside the board and total occupied cells do not overlap.
terminal_layout model ids must be optional, bounded strings and must not be treated as executable command text.
The desktop app must not show hard-coded placeholder workspaces once workspace sync is available.

safe_deploy_order: Ship rust-diffforge workspace sync and pane UI.
compatibility.notes: Existing desktop auth remains compatible. New desktop builds need the workspace endpoint to create and fetch persisted workspaces.

tests.summary: Validate policy JSON and SQL graph references.
Validate desktop workspace and pane UI.
